brand_family: Volume 2026 refresh (Rad + Lectric + Aventon + Velotric + Ride1Up)
scope_notes: NEW 2025-2026 models only. No existing v2 bike showed a verified MID-PRODUCTION running change requiring a rebuild: every 2026 shift found (XP4, XPress2, Aventure 3, Pace 4, Radster, RadRunner 2025 gen, Discover 3, Nomad 2X, Vorsa, Roadster V3) is a named successor model, consistent with v2's separate-model doctrine. Existing year ranges already reflect 2026 status (Level.2 2022-2026, Discover 2 2024-2026, RadRunner 3 Plus 2023-2026, RadWagon 5 2024-2026, XPress 750 ends 2026 = replaced by XPress2; '-present' models self-extend). An SEO-style '2026 Rad lineup' page listing RadRover 6 Plus/RadExpand 5 as current contradicts stronger dated evidence in v2 and was NOT trusted for year extensions. RAD CONTEXT: Rad Power Bikes filed Chapter 11 in December 2025, was sold at auction, and the Life EV Group acquisition completed March 5, 2026 (renamed Rad Life Mobility). The brand still operates, but parts-supply and warranty uncertainty puts a Juiced-style scarcity tailwind under working Rad takeoffs; grade Rad batteries and electronics accordingly. Not added this pass (verified to exist, thinner spec evidence): Lectric XP Trike2 (2025, 750W), base 2025 RadRunner (parts near-identical to the 2025 Plus), Aventon Level 3 / Abound SR + LR (2025, 36V ACU platform), Velotric Tempo / Summit 2 / GoMad (spring 2026), Ride1Up Vorsa base vs Lite/FT trim splits beyond tires.

bikes.motor: 500W (1092W peak, 55Nm) or 750W (1310W peak, 85Nm) geared rear hub, in-house torque sensor
bikes.year_notes: Launched spring 2025 (EBR review prices dated May 2025: 500W $999, 750W long-range $1,299), replacing the XP 3.0 as America's best-selling budget folder. Fully redesigned frame. Verified launch spec (EBR): coil fork with 50mm travel/30mm stanchions/preload+lockout (the XP line's first standard suspension fork), Star Union 602 hydraulic brakes with 2.3mm-thick 180mm rotors and aluminum levers, Shimano Altus 8-speed (11-32T, 56T chainring), 3.5in detachable full-color TFT with USB-C, 20x3.0 mixed-terrain tires with a new tread, quick-release alloy pedals, spring-supported saddle. No running changes documented through July 2026; no year splits. Do not merge XP 3.0 rows here - different frame, battery, brakes, and fork.
bikes.blurb: The 2025 successor to the XP 3.0 and the presumptive volume king. First XP with a standard suspension fork, torque sensor, hydraulics, and a color display. Too new for a used-parts market, so everything is priced at the floor until comps exist.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Lectric saddle family (EBR: spring supports + plush foam); merged with the fleet-wide row. Takeoffs list $10-25, no confirmed sold set (Jul 2026).

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Better than the XP 3.0 plank but still the first swap for all-day riders.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used; bundle filler. Merged with the shared Lectric row (31.8mm is the XP-family size).

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: 50mm of fork travel still leaves the rear rigid. Same fix as every XP.

stock_parts.comp_note: NEW part: the XP4 dropped the Wellgo folders for QR alloy flats (EBR spec). QR mechanism gives them minor standalone appeal; no solds yet (Jul 2026).

upgrade.name: Chester composite platform pedals
upgrade.note: Stock flats are fine; riders who stand on climbs still want real pins.

stock_parts.stock_part: Coil-spring fork, 50mm travel, 30mm stanchions, preload + lockout
stock_parts.comp_note: First standard suspension fork on the XP line (EBR launch spec). Folding-frame-specific fitment, thin cross-compatibility; crash part-outs will be the only supply. No solds (Jul 2026).
Same 20x3.0 size as the XP 3.0's CST but a NEW tread pattern (EBR), so kept separate from the 'CST 20x3.0 stock tire (XP)' row. Used 20x3 takeoffs run $15-20 each; priced low.

upgrade.name: Utilit Seaside 20x3.0 tire
upgrade.note: Tougher casing for the commuters who ride the XP4 daily.

stock_parts.stock_part: Star Union 602 hydraulic discs, 2-piston, 180mm x 2.3mm rotors, alloy levers
stock_parts.comp_note: SHARED NEW ROW: the 602 set is Lectric's current-generation hydraulic across XP4 and XPress2 (both verified; XPress 750 also shipped 602s per v2 notes but has no brake row to merge with). Budget hydraulic takeoff sets list $30-60; supply comes from part-outs. No solds (Jul 2026).

stock_parts.stock_part: Lectric 3.5in detachable full-color TFT with USB-C
stock_parts.comp_note: Merged with the shared 2024+ Lectric color display family per v2 convention (that row already spans USB-A and USB-C units). The XP4/XPress2 3.5in detachable unit is the newest revision within the family - note detachable + USB-C when listing. Crash-replacement demand only.
NEW row: redesigned XP4 frame means XP 3.0 pack fitment cannot be assumed - do not merge with 'Lectric XP 48V 10.4Ah battery'. Lectric packs list $150-250; priced at the low end, no solds (Jul 2026).

upgrade.name: XP4 Long-Range 48V 17.5Ah battery
upgrade.note: The 840Wh long-range pack is the headline upgrade and will feed 10.4Ah takeoffs.

stock_parts.comp_note: Standard on the $1,299 750W build, optional on the 500W (EBR). Biggest pack Lectric ships in a folder; should hold value but zero used history. Estimated $220 (Jul 2026).
Commodity Altus mech, merges with the existing shared row (XPress 750 etc.). $10-20 used everywhere.

bikes.motor: 750W geared rear hub (1310W peak), '99% quieter' motor, XPerience torque/cadence switch
bikes.year_notes: Launched May 19, 2026 at $1,399 (Electrek 2026-05-19; 9to5Toys; EBReport review), replacing the XPress 750 - v2's XPress 750 entry already flags the succession and instructs not to merge. Two frame variants at launch: high-step 'Commuter' and step-thru 'Cruiser' (frame variants, not year variants). Verified launch spec: 750W/1310W-peak quieter motor with the XPerience Switch toggling torque- vs cadence-sensor behavior, 48V 14Ah 672Wh pack, adjustable SR Suntour suspension fork, Star Union 602 hydraulic discs, Shimano Altus 8-speed, 3.5in TFT color display with USB-C and ride-customization menus. Brand new; no running changes; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: The May 2026 rework of Lectric's torque-sensor commuter. Same recipe as the XPress 750 - Suntour fork, Altus 8-speed, hydraulics - with a quieter motor and a sensor-mode switch. Zero used-parts history yet; everything priced at the floor.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Lectric saddle family; $10-25 listings, no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Daily commuters swap the saddle first. Same as every bike in this class.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used; bundle filler.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: Fork alone does not save your spine on city streets.

stock_parts.comp_note: No real used market for stock rubber grips.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Commute hands want the flat platform. Easy sell.

stock_parts.stock_part: SR Suntour adjustable coil suspension fork
stock_parts.comp_note: CARRY-OVER: launch coverage lists the same adjustable SR Suntour coil unit family the XPress 750 ran, so this merges with the existing row. Entry Suntour coils pull $40-70 used from part-outs.
Shared current-gen Lectric 602 hydraulic row (also on XP4). Budget hydraulic sets list $30-60 used; no solds.

stock_parts.stock_part: Lectric 3.5in TFT color display with USB-C
stock_parts.comp_note: Merged with the shared 2024+ Lectric color display family; the 2026 3.5in unit is the newest revision. Crash-replacement demand only.

Same 672Wh spec as the XPress 750 but the XPress2 frame is significantly updated, so cross-fitment with 'Lectric XPress 48V 14Ah battery' is NOT verified - kept separate until confirmed. Lectric packs list $150-250; priced mid-low.
Commodity Altus mech; merges with the existing shared row.

27.5in commuter wheels carried over from the XPress 750 platform, but the exact tire/tread spec on the 2026 bike is not yet pinned by reviews - kept separate from the XPress 750 tire row until confirmed identical. Generic 27.5 takeoffs run $15-25 each.

upgrade.name: Super Moto-X 27.5x2.4 tire
upgrade.note: The standard e-rated street upgrade in this size.

bikes.motor: 750W geared rear hub (1188W peak, 1440W peak in Boost; 80Nm), torque sensor
bikes.year_notes: Launched February 2025 at $1,999 (EBR/EBReport reviews; v2's Aventure.2 notes already flag it as the separate successor). Verified spec: gravity-cast frame, 750W/80Nm torque-sensor hub with a 1440W Boost mode, 36V 20Ah 733Wh keyless LG 21700 pack ejected via display or app and cross-compatible with Pace 4 / Abound SR + LR / Level 3, 80mm suspension fork, 50mm suspension seatpost standard, 26x4 fat tires, Shimano Altus 8-speed, ACU smart module (4G, GPS, geofence, remote cutoff), Apple Watch/Find My integration, 78 lb. No running changes documented through July 2026; no year splits. Do not merge Aventure.2 battery/display rows - the .3 moved to the 36V ACU platform.
bikes.blurb: Aventon's fat-tire flagship reborn on the 36V ACU smart platform. Keyless battery, torque sensor with boost, and a suspension seatpost from the factory. The shared 733Wh pack has the widest fitment Aventon has ever shipped, which will make it the money takeoff.

stock_parts.comp_note: 80mm travel verified (EBR). Maker not confirmed as the Aventure.2's Zoom unit, so kept separate from that row. Unbranded OEM forks move slowly; no solds (Jul 2026).

upgrade.name: USD-8 fat bike air fork
upgrade.note: Same story as the Aventure.2: the stock coil bounces, the USD-8 damps.

stock_parts.comp_note: SHARED NEW ROW: 50mm suspension seatpost is factory-standard on the 2025 ACU generation (verified on Aventure 3 and Pace 4). Generic suspension posts move at $25-45 used; no solds.

SHARED NEW ROW: Aventon confirms this pack is cross-compatible across Aventure 3, Pace 4, Level 3, Abound SR and Abound LR (EBR Pace 4 review) - widest fitment pool of any Aventon pack, which supports demand. Keyless eject requires a working display/app; test before crediting. New spare ~$550-600; no used solds yet (Jul 2026). Priced low.

upgrade.name: Aventure 3 spare battery 733Wh
upgrade.note: One spare pack now covers five Aventon models. Best battery investment in their lineup.

stock_parts.comp_note: SHARED NEW ROW: BC147 is the new-generation color unit on the 2025 ACU platform - explicitly verified on the Pace 4 (EBR spec sheet); the Aventure 3 runs the same new-generation display per launch coverage. Distinct from the BC280 on 2022-2025 bikes. Display also gates keyless battery eject, so replacement demand should beat BC280. No solds yet.
CARRY-OVER: same 26x4 knobby size and brand family as the Aventure.2, merges with the existing row. Street-tire conversions keep takeoff supply high.

upgrade.name: Speedster 26x4.0 street tires (pair)
upgrade.note: Knobbies hum on pavement. Speedsters cut the noise and add range.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Aventon wedge family; merges with the fleet-wide row. Minimal resale.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select saddle
upgrade.note: Still the default fix on r/aventon.

stock_parts.comp_note: Parts-bin value; merges with the fleet-wide row.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Numb hands come from round grips. Forty bucks fixes it.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared OEM platforms; merges with the fleet-wide row.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Stock pedal bearings die young on the fat bikes.

stock_parts.stock_part: Aventon hydraulic disc brake set, 180mm rotors
stock_parts.comp_note: CARRY-OVER (probable): hydraulic 180mm set continues on the Aventure 3; merged with the existing Aventure/Abound row. If teardowns later show a caliper change, split then. No used-set solds.

upgrade.name: 4-piston hydraulic brake set with 203mm rotors
upgrade.note: 78 lb at 28 mph still deserves 4-piston calipers.

stock_parts.comp_note: Altus 8-speed verified in launch coverage (upgrade from the .2's 7-speed). Commodity part; merges with the shared row.

bikes.motor: 500W geared rear hub (864W peak, 60Nm), bottom-bracket torque sensor
bikes.year_notes: Announced April 17, 2025 (v2's Pace 500.3 notes flag the succession), $1,799 MSRP, step-thru frame only - Aventon dropped the high-step and the '500' name. Verified spec (EBR review): gravity-cast aluminum frame, 500W/864W-peak/60Nm hub with double-sided BB torque sensor, 36V 20Ah 733Wh keyless pack (shared across the ACU platform), BC147 full-color display, Tektro 2-piston hydraulic discs with 180mm rotors, Innova 27.5x2.1 reflective commuter tires, Aventon 50mm suspension seatpost, rubber lock-on grips, rigid aluminum fork, display-integrated turn signals, ACU (geofence, 4G tracking, wheel lock, remote deactivation). 8-speed drivetrain; EBR's autogenerated spec table lists a GRX Di2 derailleur, an obvious scraping artifact for a $1,799 cruiser - exact derailleur model treated as unconfirmed. No running changes; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: The Pace line's 2025 ACU-era reboot: torque sensor, keyless shared battery, hydraulics, and a suspension seatpost, step-thru only. Cruiser buyers swap contact points and little else, so the shared 733Wh pack and BC147 display carry the value.

stock_parts.comp_note: Tektro 2-piston 180mm verified (EBR). Exact model not stated, so not merged with the Level.2's HD-E350 row until confirmed. New Tektro hydro sets run $85-160, capping takeoffs near $45.
Innova brand and size verified (EBR). Street tread already, so few swaps; takeoff commuter pairs move at $20-30.

upgrade.name: Marathon Plus 27.5 tires (pair)
upgrade.note: Flat protection is the only tire upgrade a cruiser needs.

stock_parts.comp_note: 8-speed confirmed; exact model unconfirmed (EBR's spec table shows a GRX Di2 artifact that cannot be right at this price - flagged, not trusted). Kept separate from the shared Altus row until a teardown confirms. Commodity value either way.
BC147 fitment explicitly verified on the Pace 4 (EBR spec sheet). Shared with the Aventure 3 row. Gates keyless battery eject, so real replacement demand; no solds yet.

Same shared ACU-platform pack as the Aventure 3 row (cross-compatibility verified by EBR: Pace 4 / Abound SR + LR / Level 3 / Aventure 3). Keyless eject needs a working display/app; test before crediting. No used solds; priced low.

upgrade.name: Pace 4 spare battery 733Wh
upgrade.note: One spare covers five Aventon models. Range anxiety sells it; takeoffs follow.

stock_parts.comp_note: CARRY-OVER: the Pace line keeps its thicker rounded cruiser seat; merges with the existing Pace row. Slightly better than wedge resale, still a saddle.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select saddle
upgrade.note: Riders past 15 miles a day still want the big spring.

stock_parts.comp_note: Merges with the fleet-wide lock-on row (EBR: 'Aventon Rubber Lockon'). Parts-bin value.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Swept bars plus GP1 wings is peak comfort cruiser cockpit.

stock_parts.comp_note: Verified standard (EBR). Shared row with the Aventure 3. Killed the old rigid-post takeoff stream - the suspension post now comes WITH the bike.
Shared OEM platforms; merges with the fleet-wide row.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Bigger platform, real grip, bearings that last.

bikes.battery: 48V 15Ah (720Wh) Safe Shield Advanced semi-integrated, Samsung 50GB/LG M50LT cells, UL 2271
bikes.year_notes: One of Rad's March 5, 2024 Safe Shield launch models (v2's RadWagon 5 notes list the Radster among the four), Rad's first new platform since 2022; EBReport review (Jan 2025) verifies: 750W/100Nm torque-sensor hub, 28 mph Class 3, 720Wh Safe Shield Advanced SEMI-INTEGRATED pack (UL 2271, Samsung 50GB or LG M50LT), Rad custom color display with six-button remote, Tektro HD-E350 hydraulics, SR Suntour 80mm hydraulic fork (tapered, thru-axle), Kenda Kwick 29x2.2 puncture-protected reflective tires, Shimano 8-speed 11-34T with 50T chainring, turn signals + brake light, ~$2,000. Still sold as of July 2026. BANKRUPTCY CONTEXT: Rad filed Chapter 11 in Dec 2025; Life EV Group completed its acquisition Mar 5, 2026 (renamed Rad Life Mobility). Brand operating, but OEM parts continuity is uncertain - a scarcity tailwind for working takeoffs. No verified running changes 2024-2026; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Rad's torque-sensor Class 3 commuter on the 2024 Safe Shield platform - the most modern Rad in the fleet. The UL-certified semi-integrated 720Wh pack and the new color display are the money parts, and the post-bankruptcy parts squeeze only helps them.

stock_parts.stock_part: Rad Safe Shield Advanced 48V 15Ah (720Wh) semi-integrated pack
stock_parts.comp_note: SHARED NEW ROW (Radster Road + Trail): SEMI-INTEGRATED Safe Shield Advanced pack - a different form factor from the RadWagon 5's EXTERNAL 'Rad Safe Shield 48V 15Ah battery (720Wh)' row, do not merge. UL 2271, Samsung 50GB/LG M50LT cells. New packs ran $479-599 at dealers pre-bankruptcy; post-Chapter 11 supply uncertainty supports demand but no used solds yet (Jul 2026). Estimated $240.

stock_parts.stock_part: Rad custom color display + six-button remote
stock_parts.comp_note: CARRY-OVER: the v2 row already names the Radster as a fitment for the 2024+ full-color Rad UI display; merges exactly. New ~$110 at dealers pre-bankruptcy; used market thin, no solds.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Tektro HD-E350 Hydraulic Brake Set (e-bike takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: HD-E350 verified (EBReport). Merges with the existing commodity HD-E350 row (Ride1Up 700/LMT'D pool): new sets $80-130, takeoff sets ~$55.

stock_parts.stock_part: SR Suntour hydraulic suspension fork, 80mm, tapered steerer, thru-axle
stock_parts.comp_note: Verified spec (EBReport). Real-brand thru-axle fork beats the old Rad coil units for resale; Suntour commuter forks trade $50-90 used. No model-specific solds.
Verified spec (EBReport). Common 29er commuter size with cross-brand demand; new Kwicks run $35-50 each. No takeoff solds; estimated $40/pair.

upgrade.name: Marathon Plus 29x2.15 tires (pair)
upgrade.note: The buy-once commuter tire in this size.

stock_parts.comp_note: SHARED ROW (Road + Trail). 8-speed 11-34T verified; exact mech model not named in reviews, so not merged with the Altus/Acera commodity rows until confirmed. Commodity value.

stock_parts.stock_part: Rad padded sport saddle with anatomic cut-out
stock_parts.comp_note: SHARED ROW (Road + Trail). Sportier cut than the classic Rad comfort saddle, so kept separate from that row. Standard saddle economics.

upgrade.name: Cloud-9 Comfort Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Upright commuters still land on the wide sprung seat.

stock_parts.comp_note: Rad has shipped Wellgo platforms fleet-wide for years; merged with the shared row on that basis, though the Radster's exact pedal is unconfirmed. Parts-lot value either way.

upgrade.name: Race Face Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Bigger platform and real pins.

bikes.battery: 48V 15Ah (720Wh) Safe Shield Advanced semi-integrated, Samsung 50GB/LG M50LT cells, UL 2271
bikes.year_notes: Sibling of the Radster Road on the March 2024 Safe Shield platform; same 750W/100Nm torque-sensor motor, same UL 2271 720Wh semi-integrated pack, same Rad color display and Tektro HD-E350 hydraulics with 180mm rotors (EBR/EBReport/99spokes). Differences: 27.5x3.0 mid-fat tires, low-rise bars, off-road geometry, suspension fork. Fork maker/travel not consistently documented across reviews - recorded conservatively below. Still sold as of July 2026 under Rad Life Mobility (post-Chapter 11 owner since Mar 2026). No verified running changes; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: The off-road half of the Radster pair: mid-fat 27.5x3 rubber and trail geometry on the same Safe Shield platform. Battery, display, and brakes pool with the Road, which is exactly what you want for inventory turnover.

stock_parts.stock_part: Rad Safe Shield Advanced 48V 15Ah (720Wh) semi-integrated pack
stock_parts.comp_note: Shared row with the Radster Road (same pack, verified both bikes). Semi-integrated form factor - do not merge with the RadWagon 5's external 720Wh row. Post-bankruptcy OEM supply uncertainty supports demand; no used solds (Jul 2026).

stock_parts.stock_part: Rad custom color display + six-button remote
stock_parts.comp_note: Carry-over; v2's shared 2024+ Rad UI row already lists the Radster. Merges exactly.

stock_parts.stock_part: Tektro HD-E350 hydraulic disc set, 180mm rotors
stock_parts.catalog_name: Tektro HD-E350 Hydraulic Brake Set (e-bike takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: HD-E350 with 180mm rotors front and rear verified (EBR Radster Trail review). Merges with the commodity HD-E350 row.

27.5x3.0 verified (EBReport calls it a 'mid-fat'); tire maker not confirmed in pulled reviews, so branded as Rad OEM for now. 27.5x3 is a useful plus-size with modest cross-demand. No solds; estimated $40/pair.

upgrade.name: Maxxis Chronicle 27.5x3.0 (pair)
upgrade.note: Name-brand plus rubber for owners who actually take it off-road.

stock_parts.comp_note: The Trail runs a suspension fork with more intent than the Road's 80mm Suntour, but pulled reviews did not pin maker or travel - recorded generically until a teardown confirms. OEM Rad-platform forks list used ~$129 asking; estimated $70.
Shared row with the Radster Road. Commodity value.
Shared row with the Radster Road.

upgrade.name: Cloud-9 Comfort Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Trail miles punish a sport saddle same as street miles.

stock_parts.comp_note: Merged with the fleet-wide Wellgo row on Rad's long-running pedal sourcing; exact unit unconfirmed.

upgrade.name: Race Face Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Real pins for wet roots and rock strikes.

bikes.year_notes: One of THREE new RadRunner models launched April 30, 2025 (Electrek: RadRunner, RadRunner Plus, RadRunner Max) - a new generation, NOT the 2020-2022 gen-1 'RadRunner Plus' already in the catalog; keep the two Plus entries strictly separate. Verified: $1,799, Safe Shield Advanced EXTERNAL 13Ah/624Wh UL-listed thermal-resistant pack (55+ mi, Range Extender compatible), color display with passcode protection + phone charging + service reminders, hydraulic disc brakes, all-terrain tires, IPX6, higher payload, standard passenger kit (foot pegs, bench seat, wheel skirt). Exact brake/tire makers not yet documented by teardowns - rows below are conservative. Sold through the Dec 2025 Chapter 11 and the Mar 2026 Life EV acquisition (Rad Life Mobility); still listed as of July 2026. No year splits.
bikes.blurb: The 2025 rebirth of Rad's moped-style runabout with a UL Safe Shield pack, hydraulics, and the passenger kit in the box. Gen-1 RadRunner parts do NOT cross over. Too new for a used market; the external Safe Shield pack is the part to watch.

stock_parts.stock_part: Rad Safe Shield Advanced 48V 13Ah (624Wh) external pack
stock_parts.comp_note: NEW row: 13Ah Safe Shield Advanced external is a new size for the 2025 RadRunner generation (Electrek 2025-04-30) - not the 14Ah legacy external, not the RadWagon 5's 15Ah. UL-listed, Range Extender ecosystem. Post-bankruptcy parts uncertainty supports demand; no used solds (Jul 2026). Estimated $200.

stock_parts.stock_part: Rad color display, passcode-protected, phone charging
stock_parts.comp_note: Merged with the 2024+ Rad UI color family: same platform display generation (color, USB charging, service reminders), now with passcode lock. If the 2025 unit proves electrically distinct, split later. No solds.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Rad hydraulic disc brake set (RadRunner 2025 gen)
stock_parts.comp_note: SHARED ROW (2025 RadRunner Plus + Max). Hydraulics verified standard across the new trio (Electrek); caliper maker not yet documented. Budget hydraulic takeoff sets list $30-60.

SHARED ROW (Plus + Max). New-generation all-terrain tread (Electrek); size class 20in verified, exact width/maker unconfirmed - do NOT merge with the gen-1 Kenda K-Rad 20x3.3 row until a teardown proves the same tire. Estimated $40/pair.

stock_parts.stock_part: Rad passenger package: bench seat, foot pegs, wheel skirt
stock_parts.comp_note: Standard on the 2025 Plus (Electrek). Rad's accessory passenger kits historically retailed $99-150; strong owner demand for bench/peg spares. New-gen fitment is its own row - gen-1 passenger parts do not cross. No solds; estimated $60.
Merged with the fleet-wide Rad comfort saddle row (Rad's utility saddles have pooled there since gen 1); exact 2025 unit unconfirmed. Saddle economics regardless.

upgrade.name: Cloud-9 Comfort Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Stop-and-go utility miles want the wide sprung seat.

stock_parts.comp_note: Merged with the fleet-wide Wellgo row; exact unit unconfirmed. Parts-lot value.

bikes.year_notes: Top of the April 30, 2025 RadRunner trio (Electrek; EBR RadRunner MAX review; 99spokes 2025): $2,299, Class 3 28 mph, 14Ah/672Wh SEMI-INTEGRATED Safe Shield pack (19-60+ mi; 120+ mi with Range Extender), and Rad's first REAR RADAR - the passcode-protected color display shows vehicles approaching from up to 100m behind with speed and distance. Hydraulic brakes, all-terrain tires, IPX6. Exact brake/tire/fork makers not yet pinned by teardowns; rows conservative. Sold through the Dec 2025 Chapter 11 / Mar 2026 Life EV Group acquisition (Rad Life Mobility); still listed July 2026. No year splits.
bikes.blurb: The radar bike. Rad's flagship utility runabout with a UL semi-integrated pack and a display that watches traffic behind you. The radar-display stack is genuinely new hardware with no aftermarket substitute - the single most interesting takeoff on any 2025 Rad.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Rad Safe Shield 48V 14Ah semi-integrated battery (RadRunner Max)
stock_parts.comp_note: Same 14Ah/672Wh electrical spec as the legacy 2021-platform semi-integrated pack, but a NEW Safe Shield generation on a new frame - fitment with the old 'Rad 48V 14Ah semi-integrated battery pack' row is NOT verified, kept separate. UL-listed. No used solds (Jul 2026); estimated $220.

NEW part: first Rad display integrated with the rear radar module (100m vehicle detection with speed/distance readout - EBR). Unique hardware, no aftermarket equivalent, so crash replacements should command real money once bikes age. Zero market history; estimated $70 conservatively.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Rad hydraulic disc brake set (RadRunner 2025 gen)
stock_parts.comp_note: Shared row with the 2025 RadRunner Plus. Hydraulics verified standard; maker unconfirmed.
Shared row with the 2025 Plus; exact width/maker unconfirmed, do not merge with gen-1 K-Rad rows.
Merged with the fleet-wide Rad comfort saddle row; exact 2025 unit unconfirmed.

upgrade.name: Cloud-9 Comfort Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: The standard first-week fix.

stock_parts.comp_note: Merged with the fleet-wide Wellgo row; exact unit unconfirmed.

bikes.motor: 750W (1100W peak) geared rear hub, 75Nm, SensorSwap torque/cadence
bikes.year_notes: Launched February 2026 at $1,999 (Ebike Escape review; Velotric launch blog; part of Velotric's five-model spring 2026 push with Tempo, Summit 2, GoMad, Nomad 2X) - v2's Discover 2 notes already flag it as the separate successor. Verified spec: 750W/1100W/75Nm with SensorSwap dual sensors, 730Wh Samsung/LG 21700 pack (up from 705.6Wh - a DIFFERENT pack from the Discover 2/Summit 1/Nomad 2 706Wh SKU, do not merge), 80mm AIR suspension fork with 32mm stanchions, weight-adjustable air chambers, rebound, thru-axle and lockout, Kenda 27.5x2.4 mixed-terrain tires, Shimano Acera 8-speed with widened 11-40T cassette (46T ring), suspension seatpost shared with the Nomad family, dual-spring saddle, 500-lumen headlight, NFC card unlock, Apple Find My/Android FindHub, 440 lb capacity, low step-thru. Brand new; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Velotric's best-seller rebuilt for 2026 with a real air fork at $1,999 - unheard of in this class. The bigger 730Wh pack breaks compatibility with the old three-model battery pool, so grade it as its own part from day one.

stock_parts.comp_note: NEW pack, capacity up from the 706Wh SKU that pooled across Discover 2/Summit 1/Nomad 2 - cross-fit NOT verified, kept separate. UL-era Velotric packs hold value but zero used history (Jul 2026). Estimated $220.

upgrade.name: Velotric spare battery 730Wh
upgrade.note: Proprietary tray, OEM spare is the only play. All battery sales final per Velotric.

stock_parts.stock_part: Air suspension fork, 80mm travel, 32mm stanchions, rebound + lockout, thru-axle
stock_parts.comp_note: The headline 2026 upgrade (Ebike Escape): a genuine air fork with rebound adjust at a commuter price. Air units clear more than coils used - but OEM fitment and zero history cap it. Estimated $60.
Verified Kenda 27.5x2.4 (Ebike Escape). Common commuter size; takeoff pairs move at $20-30.

upgrade.name: Super Moto-X 27.5x2.4 tire (pair)
upgrade.note: E-rated street casing; the standard upgrade in this size.

stock_parts.comp_note: Acera verified (Ebike Escape); merges with the existing commodity Acera row. $15-20 eBay commodity.

stock_parts.stock_part: Suspension seatpost with preload (shared with Nomad family)
stock_parts.comp_note: SHARED NEW ROW: Ebike Escape confirms the Discover 3 seatpost is the same unit the Nomad line runs; also used on the Nomad 2X entry. Generic suspension posts move $25-45 used.
New dual-spring seat for 2026 (launch materials). Slightly better than flat-saddle resale; still a saddle.

stock_parts.stock_part: Velotric color display, 2026 software (Ride Tuning, NFC unlock)
stock_parts.comp_note: SHARED NEW ROW (Discover 3 + Nomad 2X): the 2026 models ship a complete software overhaul with NFC security cards, so treated as a new display generation distinct from the D2/S1/T1/N2 3.5in SKU. Exact hardware ID unconfirmed - split later if the 2026 bikes prove to use different units. Factory replacements will cap price.

Hydraulics are platform-standard on Discover since the D2, but the pulled Discover 3 review did not name the caliper - recorded conservatively, not merged with any Tektro row. Budget hydro sets list $30-60 used.

bikes.year_notes: Part of Velotric's spring 2026 five-model expansion (Velotric PR; electricbikeexplorer 2026 lineup coverage). Verified headline spec: 750W/1400W-peak/105Nm hub, 801.6Wh pack, up to 75 mi, DUAL air suspension - 120mm air fork plus DNM rear shock - Kenda 26x4.0 fat tires, Apple Find My, step-thru and step-over frames. Successor to the Nomad line above the existing Nomad 2 (which continues the 706Wh shared pack). Detail teardowns are scarce this early: brake maker, drivetrain, and display hardware IDs unconfirmed - rows below are deliberately conservative. No year splits.
bikes.blurb: Velotric goes full suspension: 120mm air fork, DNM shock, and the biggest pack the brand has shipped. A budget full-sus fat bike will get ridden hard, which means crash part-outs - the fork, shock, and 802Wh pack are the parts with a future.

stock_parts.comp_note: Biggest Velotric pack to date; model-specific, no cross-fit verified with any other SKU. Zero used history (Jul 2026); estimated $240.

upgrade.name: Velotric spare battery 801.6Wh
upgrade.note: Full-power fat-bike duty drains packs fast. OEM spare or nothing.

stock_parts.comp_note: 120mm air travel verified in lineup coverage. Fat-bike air forks have real cross-brand demand ($60-120 used for name units); OEM branding caps it. Estimated $80.
DNM verified in lineup coverage; exact model TBD. DNM coil/air shocks trade $40-80 used with steady budget full-sus demand. Estimated $60.
Kenda 26x4.0 verified. Exact tread model unconfirmed, so not merged with other Kenda 26x4 rows yet. Used fat pairs move at $35-45.

upgrade.name: Speedster 26x4.0 street tires (pair)
upgrade.note: The pavement-conversion classic for any 26x4 fat bike.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared 2026-generation row with the Discover 3; hardware ID unconfirmed, split later if needed.
Hydraulics assumed platform-standard at this weight/speed; maker and rotor size unconfirmed in pulled coverage. Conservative row, no merge.
Shared row with the Discover 3 (Ebike Escape ties the family seatpost together). Fitment on the 2X specifically is probable but unconfirmed.

bikes.motor: AKM angled-gear 750W rear hub (1380W peak, 95Nm), Intui-Drive torque + cadence sensors (switchable)
bikes.year_notes: Launched April 2025 at $1,595 (EBR review) as the successor to the discontinued 700 Series - v2's 700 entry already records 'discontinued early 2025 in favor of the Vorsa'. Verified spec (EBR): AKM angled-gear 750W/1380W/95Nm with switchable Intui-Drive torque + R1CBC cadence sensing, 48V 15Ah Samsung 50GB UL 2271 pack with lockable integrated mount, 100mm coil fork with hydraulic lockout + preload, Star Union 2-piston hydraulics (180mm front / 203mm rear, 2.3mm rotors), Shimano Acera 8-speed (Altus trigger, 11-34T, 42T ring), Schwalbe Smart Sam 27.5x2.6 tires, DM07 full-color TOUCHSCREEN display, Velo Iron Bow saddle, 150 lb rear rack (181 lb with Connect+ extension). 2026 model year added trims: Vorsa Lite ($1,495, lighter build, 27.5x2.2 tires per Tom's Guide/OutdoorGearLab) and Vorsa FT ($1,695, 26x4 Kenda Krusade fat tires) - trim variants, not year revisions; this entry covers the standard build, note trim on intake. No mid-production changes found; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Ride1Up's do-everything replacement for the 700 Series: torque-and-cadence switchable, touchscreen display, and a UL Samsung pack under $1,600. Name-brand rubber and the touchscreen give its takeoffs more life than the usual budget fare.

stock_parts.comp_note: Model-specific lockable integrated pack; no cross-fit with 700/Turris/LMT'D packs. Samsung 50GB cells + UL cert help resale; zero used history (Jul 2026). Estimated $190.

upgrade.name: Vorsa Spare Battery (OEM)
upgrade.note: Sold direct on ride1up.com. OEM or nothing on a frame-locked pack.

stock_parts.stock_part: 100mm coil fork with hydraulic lockout and preload
stock_parts.comp_note: Unbranded OEM coil unit, same economics as the 700 Series Mozo: $30 repair part, no aftermarket pull.

upgrade.name: SR Suntour XCM34 27.5
upgrade.note: Cheapest fork swap that actually feels different.

stock_parts.stock_part: Star Union 2-piston hydraulic set, 180mm front / 203mm rear, 2.3mm rotors
stock_parts.catalog_name: Star Union Hydraulic Brake Set (Vorsa takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Verified spec (EBR). Mixed-rotor budget hydraulics; kept separate from the Lectric 602 row (different set/config). Takeoff sets $30-60; no solds.
Merges with the existing commodity Acera row (700 Series pool). $15-20 commodity.
Real-name rubber (verified EBR, standard build; Lite runs 27.5x2.2, FT runs 26x4 Kenda Krusade - note trim on intake). New Smart Sams $40-55 each; lightly worn pair ~$40. No solds.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon Plus MTB 27.5 (pair)
upgrade.note: The flat-proof forever tire for daily duty.

stock_parts.comp_note: Touchscreen with PAS/speed/torque tuning menus (EBR) - a step above the KD-class units, and crash replacements have no aftermarket substitute. Estimated $35; no solds.
Velo-branded but still a stock saddle; $15 territory. Not the Selle Royal pool from the 700/LMT'D - kept separate.

upgrade.name: Cloud-9 Cruiser Select
upgrade.note: Same story as every commuter: stock saddle lasts until the first long weekend.

stock_parts.comp_note: No resale market for stock grips; merges with the fleet-wide row.

upgrade.name: Ergon GP1
upgrade.note: The grip everyone lands on after mile ten.

stock_parts.comp_note: 150 lb rating (181 lb with the Connect+ extension) is unusually high for the class; modular ecosystem should create accessory demand. No solds; estimated $30.

bikes.motor: 500W angled-gear rear hub, 50Nm, Intui-Drive torque sensor
bikes.year_notes: Launched late 2024 at $1,495, later $1,295 (v2's Roadster V2 notes record the succession; Electrek/EBR/Tom's Guide coverage). A genuinely different bike from the V2: 500W/50Nm angled-gear motor with the Bosch-pedigree Intui-Drive torque sensor, REMOVABLE 360Wh Samsung GB50 pack (the V2's was sealed in-frame - a takeoff that literally could not exist before), and two drivetrain trims: single-speed DAYCO Power Carbon belt (Dayco's e-bike debut, not Gates) or 9-speed microShift Advent chain drive. 28 mph pedal assist. Trim variants, not year revisions. Sold into 2026 (99spokes lists 2026 model years incl. a Low-Step). No mid-production changes found; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: The stealth Roadster grows up: torque sensor, removable Samsung pack, and a Dayco belt instead of Gates. The removable battery finally gives this line a battery takeoff, and the Advent trim adds a real derailleur to the pool.

stock_parts.comp_note: First removable Roadster pack (V2 was sealed). Small 360Wh size caps value; Samsung cells and model-specific fit prop it up for Roadster owners. No solds (Jul 2026); estimated $150.

upgrade.name: Roadster V3 Spare Battery (OEM)
upgrade.note: Small pack, small range. The spare doubles it.

stock_parts.stock_part: Dayco Power Carbon belt drive (single-speed trim)
stock_parts.catalog_name: Dayco Power Carbon Belt Drive (Roadster V3 takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Dayco, NOT Gates - do not pool with the V2's Gates CDN row. Dayco's e-bike debut means thin spares channels, which cuts both ways: little demand yet, but no OEM alternative either. Estimated $25.
Chain-trim only; merges with the existing Advent commodity row (Prodigy pool). Advent has a cult following; ~$35 used.
700c street rubber; exact width not pinned in pulled coverage (V2 ran 700x28c). Generic used 700c pairs are yard-sale goods.

upgrade.name: Continental Gatorskin 700x32 (pair)
upgrade.note: City glass eats skinny stock tires. Gatorskins end it.

stock_parts.comp_note: Unbranded slim saddle, effectively no resale; V3 unit not confirmed identical to the V2's, kept separate.

upgrade.name: Brooks Cambium C17
upgrade.note: The saddle the fixie-adjacent Roadster crowd actually buys.

stock_parts.comp_note: No resale market; merges with the fleet-wide row.

upgrade.name: Ergon GP1
upgrade.note: Flat-bar city bike, long rides, numb hands. Same cure.

stock_parts.comp_note: Repair-part demand from other V3 owners only; hardware not confirmed shared with the V2's compact LCD, kept separate. Estimated $15.
